creator: rnejdl@ringofsaturn.com
text:
On Fri, 13 Aug 2010 12:11:50 -0400, "J.R. Oldroyd" <fbsd@opal.com>
wrote:
> On Fri, 13 Aug 2010 11:38:47 -0400, "J.R. Oldroyd" <fbsd@opal.com> wrote:
>>
>> On Fri, 13 Aug 2010 10:07:41 -0500, Rusty Nejdl <rnejdl@ringofsaturn.com> wrote:
>> >
>> > On Fri, 13 Aug 2010 08:52:05 -0400, "J.R. Oldroyd" <fbsd@opal.com>
>> > wrote:
>> > > On Fri, 13 Aug 2010 14:08:04 +0200, Pav Lucistnik <pav@FreeBSD.org> wrote:
>> > >>
>> > >> Dear maintainer of FreeBSD port mail/assp, please take a look at
>> > >>
>> > >>    http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=149445
>> > >>
>> > >> Do you approve this update?
>> > >>
>> > >
>> > > Hi Pav, Rusty,
>> > >
>> > > Rusty, as I mentioned in my reply to you the other day, I've not been
>> > > able to make the time to get to this for a while.  So thanks for doing
>> > > the update.
>> > >
>> > > Would you be willing to take over maintainership of this port for a
>> > > while, please?
>> > >
>> > > If so, please also update the MAINTAINER line, too.
>> > >
>> > > Thanks,
>> > > 	-jr
>> >
>> > JR,
>> >
>> > Sure, I'll take over maintainership of this port as well.  I also need
>> > to submit a new diff as it looks like he re-rolled the zip file again as
>> > the size changed.
>> >
>> > Rusty
>>
>> Ah - he's still doing that, too.  I've asked him several times
>> to not do that.  He doesn't make proper releases.  His "releases"
>> are just his current snapshot and may contain bugs and/or
>> regressions.  Often a "release" is re-rolled if issues are brought
>> to his attention and he feels few enough people have downloaded it!!
>> Other times a "release" is very quickly followed by another "release"
>> with a new version number.
>>
>> You may want to subscribe to the assp-test mailing list.  Only
>> consider a port-update if the discussion suggests a "release" may
>> actually be stable.  You may also want to email him when you're doing
>> a port update, to specifically ask that he not re-roll that particular
>> dist file any more.
>>
>> But anyway, thanks for taking it over for a while.
>>
>> 	-jr
> 
> FWIW, the ASSP web site:
> 	http://assp.sourceforge.net/
> still lists version 1.7.1.3 as the latest "release".
> 
> 	-jr

Which is funny because the download site has 1.7.5.7 as the top
download:

http://sourceforge.net/projects/assp/files/

Anyways, here's the updated patch that fixed the distinfo file as well
as the depency on p5-IO-Sockets-INET6.  The upgrade still isn't pretty
so I will work on that as I have time but it's working as well as it
does now.

Rusty
